title: maharanga fistulosa
intro: Maharanga fistulosa, also known as the 'Dhatura', is a poisonous plant native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant contains medicinal properties due to the presence of alkaloids like scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which have been used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Maharanga fistulosa include its use in treating asthma, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, but it is essential to use it under the guidance of a qualified practitioner due to its toxic nature.
